rationale: Built the branch binary (62cd6df3) and drove `validate spec` against 8 hand-built fixtures under $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp (not the checked-in tests) covering every R5/R6 case in my lens: (1) path-like zero-owner Impacted-Domains entry -> impacted-domains-resolve, severity 0 (SevError); (2) path-like multi-owner (ambiguous, alpha+beta both claiming internal/foo/**) -> impacted-domains-resolve naming both owners, SevError; (3) bare-name-no-manifest -> no issue, passes; (4) bare-ID anchor [ADR-9999](...) to a nonexistent ADR -> adr-touchpoint-missing, SevError; (5) filename-form anchor [ADR-9999-x.md](...) to a nonexistent ADR -> adr-touchpoint-missing, SevError (both anchor forms independently confirmed); (6) filename-form anchor to an EXISTING ADR-0031 -> passes; (7) bare-prose 'ADR-9999' mention with no anchor -> passes despite the ID not existing (existence-only+anchored-only boundary holds); (8) a 110-shaped spec mixing real anchors (bare+filename-form) and prose mentions of nonexistent ADRs -> passes cleanly. Across all 8 runs the only other issue ever emitted was the pre-existing 'lifecycle-binding' warning; no adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant ever leaked (R7c boundary holds empirically, not just by code inspection). Results match the diff's stated behavior and the checked-in spec_test.go assertions exactly, but independently reproduced via a fresh binary build and fixtures I authored myself rather than trusting the shipped test file. No discrepancies found.
findings: Confirmed empirically (branch binary, not just test-file inspection): path-like zero-owner and multi-owner Impacted-Domains entries both surface impacted-domains-resolve at SevError, matching plan-approve's normalizeImpactedDomains behavior byte-for-byte, including the ambiguity message naming both conflicting owners.

Confirmed both ADR-Touchpoint anchor forms (bare-ID `[ADR-9999](...)` and filename-form `[ADR-9999-x.md](...)`) independently trigger adr-touchpoint-missing at SevError when the target ADR does not exist, and both pass cleanly when it does.

Confirmed the existence-only/anchored-only boundary holds under test: a bare-prose ADR-#### mention with no markdown anchor never triggers adr-touchpoint-missing even when the referenced ID does not exist, and no adr-coverage-*/adr-cite-irrelevant diagnostic ever appeared across any of the 8 fixtures.

A 110-shaped realistic spec (real anchors of both forms + prose mentions of not-yet-created ADR IDs) validates cleanly end-to-end, consistent with the plan's stated goal of parity without new false positives.
